title: gagea graeca
intro: Gagea graeca, also known as Daphne or Greek Gagea, is a perennial plant species belonging to the lily family (Liliaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Gagea graeca include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate congestion, and promote the discharge of mucus. Herbal preparations of Gagea graeca typically involve drying and grinding the plant's rhizomes, which can be made into teas, infusions, or added to tinctures, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional.
